items.title: Poetry as Gift: Eucharistic Miracle
items.summary: Explores the relationship between poetry and Eucharistic spirituality, examining how literary art can serve as a vehicle for theological reflection and wonder.
items.ggiConnection: Strong alignment with GGI's emphasis on beauty, liturgy, and the integration of faith and culture through the arts.

items.title: Ratzinger in the Whirlwind
items.summary: Examination of Benedict XVI's theological and intellectual contributions during turbulent times in the Church, exploring his role as a leading Catholic intellectual.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to Catholic intellectual tradition and theological formation that GGI promotes.

items.title: Declinism Is Easy: Art, Beauty, and the Need to Make it New
items.summary: Argues against cultural pessimism while advocating for authentic artistic renewal, emphasizing the need for creative engagement rather than mere critique of decline.
items.ggiConnection: Directly connects to GGI themes of beauty, cultural renewal, and the importance of creative engagement in formation.

items.title: Forming Families to Sing With Angels
items.summary: Explores formation of families in liturgical singing and sacred music traditions, emphasizing the role of domestic church in transmitting beauty and worship.
items.ggiConnection: Strongly aligns with GGI themes of liturgy, beauty, family formation, and cultural transmission.

items.title: Against Concelebration: The Remarkable Intervention of Archbishop Paul-Pierre Philippe OP at Vatican II
items.summary: Historical analysis of Archbishop Philippe's intervention against concelebration during Vatican II, offering substantive liturgical theology and historical context.
items.ggiConnection: Directly relevant to GGI's liturgical formation and Catholic intellectual tradition emphasis

items.title: The Need for Non-Ironic Limits: A Review of The Philosophy of Philip Rieff
items.summary: Scholarly review of Philip Rieff's philosophy examining cultural limits and therapeutic culture, offering deep intellectual analysis of contemporary cultural challenges. Strong mission alignment on faith, reason, and cultural critique.
items.ggiConnection: Strong alignment with GGI's cultural renewal mission and intellectual tradition

items.title: How To Truly KNOW Something | Dr. Trent Dougherty
items.summary: Philosopher Dr. Trent Dougherty discusses epistemology and the nature of knowledge in Catholic philosophical framework - deep intellectual content aligned with faith and reason.
items.ggiConnection: Exemplifies GGI's commitment to Catholic intellectual tradition and philosophical inquiry

items.title: Why Modern Storytelling Is SO BAD | Dr. Martin Shaw
items.summary: Dr. Martin Shaw critiques modern storytelling's decline and explores narrative's role in cultural formation - substantial analysis connecting literature, culture, and meaning.
items.ggiConnection: Directly aligns with GGI's focus on cultural renewal, Great Books tradition, and the power of story in formation

items.title: What's the Purpose of Life? | Contemplata Bonus Episode
items.summary: Fundamental philosophical question addressed from Thomistic perspective. Engages core existential questions through Catholic intellectual framework.
items.ggiConnection: Directly aligns with GGI's Great Books approach and fundamental philosophical questions

items.title: How Science Killed Materialism
items.summary: Explores how modern scientific discoveries challenge materialist worldview, offering philosophical analysis of the intersection between faith and reason in contemporary scientific discourse.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's emphasis on faith-reason integration and Catholic intellectual tradition

items.title: God and Man at MIT
items.summary: Examination of faith life and intellectual challenges at MIT, exploring the relationship between Catholic belief and academic excellence in secular institutions.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to GGI's mission of integrating faith and reason in higher education

items.title: The Word Became Flesh and Picked Up a Hammer
items.summary: Theological reflection on the dignity of work and manual labor through Incarnational lens, exploring Catholic understanding of vocation and craftsmanship.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to GGI's emphasis on beauty, craftsmanship, and incarnational spirituality

items.title: The West Distorted
items.summary: Analysis of how Western civilization has been misrepresented or misunderstood, likely examining cultural and intellectual distortions in contemporary discourse.
items.ggiConnection: Directly aligns with GGI's mission of cultural renewal and understanding the Western intellectual tradition.

items.title: “Ecclesiacide,” Then and Now
items.summary: Historical and contemporary analysis of attacks on the Church's institutional integrity, drawing parallels between past and present challenges to ecclesial life.
items.ggiConnection: Relates to GGI's focus on Church renewal and understanding Catholic institutional life.

items.title: Thinking Christianly About Technology Again: Technology and the Fragmentation of Society
items.summary: Christian philosophical analysis of how technology contributes to social fragmentation, offering theological perspectives on contemporary digital challenges.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's concern with cultural renewal and the need for thoughtful Christian engagement with modernity.

items.title: Saint Louis de Montfort and his “short, perfect and sure way” to Christ
items.summary: Thoughtful exploration of St. Louis de Montfort's Marian spirituality and path to Christ, offering solid spiritual and intellectual content.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's Catholic intellectual tradition and formation focus

items.title: Seeing Woman through the Eyes of Gertrud von le Fort
items.summary: Literary analysis exploring the Catholic understanding of womanhood through the works of German Catholic author Gertrud von le Fort.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to GGI's Great Books tradition and Catholic intellectual heritage

items.title: Two Royal Psalters
items.summary: Examines historical royal psalters as examples of sacred art and liturgical manuscript tradition, showcasing intersection of beauty, worship, and cultural heritage.
items.ggiConnection: Connects to GGI's emphasis on liturgy, beauty, and preservation of Catholic cultural treasures.

items.title: The Eastertide Hymns of the Apostles
items.summary: Analysis of Eastertide hymns attributed to the Apostles, exploring early Christian liturgical poetry and its theological significance for Easter celebration.
items.ggiConnection: Aligns with GGI's liturgy and beauty themes, connecting ancient tradition to contemporary worship.

items.title: The Solemnity of St Joseph, Patron of the Universal Church 2026
items.summary: Coverage of the upcoming Solemnity of St. Joseph in 2026, examining liturgical preparations and theological significance of this patronal feast.
items.ggiConnection: Strong connection to GGI's liturgical formation focus and emphasis on Catholic traditions
